landing_page_description: A toolbox of cognitive assessment instruments for measuring attention, memory, and processing speed
landing_header_description: A collection of standardized neuropsychological tests for measuring cognitive abilities
disclaimer_text: Note: These tests are for educational and informational purposes only and are not meant for clinical diagnosis.
search_tests_placeholder: Search tests by name or description...
no_tests_found: No tests found matching your criteria.
cta_add_own_text: Contribute to the toolbox! Researchers can sign up to add and manage their own cognitive tests within this platform.
corsi_description: Assesses visuo-spatial short-term working memory capacity. Participants observe and reproduce increasingly complex sequences of highlighted blocks, measuring spatial memory span and sequential processing abilities.
pvt_description: Evaluates sustained attention and alertness by measuring reaction time to visual stimuli. A gold standard for assessing vigilance deficits, fatigue effects, and circadian rhythm disruptions.
tol_description: Measures executive function, planning, and problem-solving abilities. Participants rearrange colored balls on pegs to match target configurations, requiring strategic thinking and forward planning.
rpm_description: A culture-fair intelligence test measuring non-verbal reasoning, abstract thinking, and pattern recognition. Participants identify missing pieces in visual matrix patterns of increasing complexity.
vm_description: Evaluates visual recognition memory using abstract patterns. Participants study visual stimuli and later identify them among distractors, measuring encoding and retrieval of non-verbal visual information.
gng_sst_description: Assesses response inhibition, impulse control, and cognitive flexibility. Measures the ability to execute or suppress motor responses based on visual cues, evaluating inhibitory control mechanisms.
akt_description: A visual cancellation task measuring sustained attention, concentration, and visual processing speed. Participants identify and mark target symbols among distractors, assessing selective attention and accuracy.
wtb_description: Measures auditory short-term memory and verbal working memory capacity. Participants listen to and reproduce sequences of spoken digits in forward and backward order, assessing phonological loop function.
proposal_info_p1: Please upload a PDF containing details about the test: description, methodology, target population, scoring, and any existing validation data or references.
proposal_info_p2: Administrators will review your submission. You can only have one pending proposal at a time.
landscape_hint: Please rotate your device to landscape mode for the best experience.
contact_cta_text: Questions, ideas, or collaboration? Reach out to us.
about_page_subtitle: Open-source cognitive assessments — standardized, accessible, and ready for the digital age.
about_why_p1: Cognitive testing still relies heavily on pen-and-paper procedures, proprietary software with restrictive licenses, or lab setups that require participants to be physically present. This creates barriers for everyone involved: participants must travel, researchers spend time on manual scoring, and results are harder to compare across studies.
about_why_p2: psyKasten is built to change that. We provide a free, open-source toolbox of standardized neuropsychological tests that run in any modern browser — on tablets, laptops, and phones. No installation, no special hardware, no cost.
about_researchers_1_text: Every participant sees the same instructions, timing, and stimuli. No experimenter variability.
about_researchers_2_text: Results are captured automatically with millisecond-precise reaction times, trial-level data, and computed scores. No manual transcription.
about_researchers_3_text: Create studies, assign tests to participants via unique links, track completion, and export data — all from a single dashboard.
about_researchers_4_text: Tests are available in German, English, and Spanish, with an extensible translation system.
about_participants_1_text: Participants receive a link and start immediately. Nothing to install, no sign-up required.
about_participants_2_text: Optimized for tablets and touch screens, but equally usable on desktop.
about_participants_3_text: Each test includes a tutorial, animated demo, and practice round before the real assessment begins.
about_participants_4_text: Clear visuals, keyboard navigation, screen-reader labels, and responsive layouts.
about_tests_p1: The toolbox currently includes tests for spatial working memory (Corsi), sustained attention (PVT), executive planning (Tower of London), non-verbal reasoning (Raven's Progressive Matrices), response inhibition (Go/NoGo & Stop-Signal), attention & concentration (AKT), and choice reaction (WTB) — with more on the way.
about_tests_p2_suffix: to be added to the platform.
about_who_p1: psyKasten is developed by researchers who got tired of the status quo. We believe cognitive assessment tools should be open, reproducible, and available to anyone who needs them — whether you run a university lab, teach a psychology seminar, or conduct clinical screenings.
about_who_p2: The project is open source and built with Next.js, React, and Prisma. Contributions, feedback, and test proposals are always welcome.
about_contact_text: Questions, ideas, or feedback? We'd love to hear from you.
privacy_2_text: This website processes personal data exclusively to the extent technically necessary. No data is collected for advertising or tracking purposes. The legal basis is Art. 6(1)(b) and (f) GDPR (performance of a contract and legitimate interests in operating the website).
privacy_3_text: This website is hosted by Hetzner Online GmbH (Industriestr. 25, 91710 Gunzenhausen, Germany). Hetzner operates data centers exclusively in Germany and Finland (EU). When you visit the website, the following technical data is automatically recorded by the web server (server log files):
privacy_3_item_date: Date and time of the request
privacy_3_text2: This data is technically necessary to deliver the website and is not combined with other data sources. The legal basis is Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR.
privacy_4_intro: This website uses only technically necessary cookies:
privacy_4_session_text: (NextAuth session token) — Set upon login and necessary to identify you as a logged-in user. Expires at the end of the session or when the token expires.
privacy_4_csrf_text: — Protects forms against cross-site request forgery attacks. Purely technically necessary.
privacy_4_locale_text: (NEXT_LOCALE) — Stores your selected language so the site is displayed in the correct language.
privacy_4_theme: Additionally, your selected color scheme (light/dark) is stored in your browser's Local Storage. This is not personal data and does not leave your browser.
privacy_4_no_tracking: No advertising or tracking cookies are set. A cookie banner is therefore not required.
privacy_4a_text: This website uses Umami, a privacy-friendly, open-source web analytics tool, to collect anonymized usage statistics. Umami does not use cookies, does not collect or store any personal data (such as IP addresses or browser fingerprints), and does not track individual visitors across sessions or websites. The data collected is limited to aggregated, non-personal information such as page views, referrer URLs, browser type, and device type. This data is used exclusively to understand general usage patterns and improve the user experience. The analytics instance is self-hosted on our own infrastructure in Germany. Since no personal data is processed and no cookies are set, no consent is required (Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR — legitimate interest in improving the website).
privacy_5_intro: Researchers can register with an email address and password or via Google OAuth. The following data is stored:
privacy_5_item_password: Password hash (for password registration; the plaintext password is not stored)
privacy_5_item_google: For Google login: profile data provided by Google (name, email, profile picture URL)
privacy_5_text: This data is necessary to provide you access to the research dashboard (Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR). You can delete your account at any time via the account settings in your dashboard. This permanently removes all your data, including studies, participants, and results.
privacy_6_intro: When participants complete a cognitive test via an assigned link, the following data is stored:
privacy_6_item_id: Participant identifier (assigned by the researcher, e.g., pseudonym or code)
privacy_6_item_meta: Optional metadata (e.g., age group, group), if configured by the researcher
privacy_6_item_results: Test results (reaction times, scores, response sequences)
privacy_6_text: No IP addresses or browser fingerprints of participants are stored. Processing is based on the legitimate interest in conducting the study (Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR). The responsibility for obtaining informed consent from participants lies with the respective researcher.
privacy_7_p1: Personal data is not shared with, sold to, or used for advertising purposes by third parties. An exception is the hosting provider, which technically stores the data as part of order processing (Art. 28 GDPR).
privacy_7_p2_prefix: When using Google login, data is exchanged with Google as part of the OAuth process. For more information, see
privacy_8_intro: Under the GDPR, you have the following rights:
privacy_8_access_text: — What data is stored about you
privacy_8_erasure_text: — Deletion of your data ("right to be forgotten")
privacy_8_portability_text: — Provision of your data in a machine-readable format
privacy_8_complaint: You also have the right to lodge a complaint with a data protection supervisory authority.
privacy_9_text: The connection to this website is TLS-encrypted (HTTPS). Passwords are stored exclusively as cryptographic hashes (bcrypt). Access to study data is protected by authentication and role-based access control. API endpoints are secured by CSRF protection and rate limiting.
privacy_10_text: For questions about data protection, you can reach us at:
privacy_11_text: This privacy policy may be updated as needed. The current version is always available on this page.
terms_1_text: These Terms of Use govern your use of the psyKasten platform (hereinafter "Platform"). By registering for or using the Platform, you agree to these terms.
terms_2_text: psyKasten provides a free, open-source toolbox of standardized cognitive tests. Researchers can create studies, assign participants, and export results. Participants complete cognitive tests via assigned links.
terms_3_text: Use of the researcher dashboard requires registration with a valid email address. You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your login credentials. Accounts may be suspended for violations of these terms.
terms_4_item_1: Use the Platform only for lawful purposes
terms_4_item_2: Not submit false or misleading information
terms_4_item_3: Not use the Platform in any way that disrupts its operation
terms_4_item_4: As a researcher, obtain informed consent from your participants
terms_4_item_5: Comply with applicable data protection laws when collecting and processing study data
terms_5_text: The Platform is open-source software. Test content and design are protected by copyright. Users may freely use test results and exported data for their research purposes.
terms_6_text: The Platform is provided "as is" without warranty of any kind. The tests are intended for research and educational purposes only and are not designed for clinical diagnosis. We are not liable for data loss, service interruptions, or decisions made based on test results.
terms_7_text_prefix: Data processing is governed by our
terms_7_text_suffix: . By using the Platform, you consent to the data processing described therein.
terms_8_text: You may delete your account at any time by contacting us via email. We reserve the right to suspend or terminate accounts that violate these terms.
terms_9_text: We may modify these Terms of Use at any time. Continued use of the Platform after changes constitutes acceptance of the updated terms. The current version is always available on this page.
terms_10_text: For questions about these Terms of Use, you can reach us at:

res_banner_text: Create studies, assign tests to participants, and export structured data — all from one dashboard.
res_page_subtitle: Everything you need to run standardized cognitive assessments — from study setup to data export.
res_how_intro: psyKasten is designed to get you from idea to data as quickly as possible. Here's the typical workflow:
res_step_signup_text: Sign up with your email or Google account. You'll get immediate access to the researcher dashboard.
res_step_create_study_text: Name your study, add a description, and select one or more cognitive tests. Each study acts as a container for participants and results.
res_step_add_participants_text: Add participants individually or use bulk import (paste a list of identifiers). Optionally attach metadata like age, group, or handedness.
res_step_share_links_text: Each participant gets a unique, secure link. Share it via email, QR code, or any messenger. No participant account needed.
res_step_collect_results_text: Results are captured automatically as participants complete tests — reaction times, scores, and full trial-level data.
res_step_export_text: Export data as structured CSV or JSON. Filter by study, test type, or date range. Ready for SPSS, R, Python, or Excel.
res_feat_study_mgmt_text: Create, edit, duplicate, and delete studies. Each study holds its own participants, assignments, and results.
res_feat_participants_text: Single or bulk import. Attach metadata fields (age, gender, group, custom fields). Search and filter participants.
res_feat_links_text: Every participant gets a unique, one-time-use link. Share via email or QR code. Links can be regenerated if needed.
res_feat_analytics_text: Track completion rates, view score distributions, and see recent activity — all in real time on your study page.
res_feat_export_text: Export as structured CSV (one row per participant) or raw CSV/JSON with full trial data. Choose which fields to include.
res_feat_i18n_text: All tests are available in German, English, and Spanish. Participants see the test in their browser language.
res_test_flow_intro: Every test follows a standardized flow so participants know exactly what to expect:
res_test_flow_note: Practice rounds include real-time feedback. The actual test runs in fullscreen with no distractions.
res_data_text: psyKasten is designed with data minimization in mind. We only store what's needed for the research:
res_data_no_ip: No IP addresses of participants are stored
res_data_no_tracking: No cookies, analytics, or tracking for participants
res_data_pseudonym: Participants are identified by researcher-assigned codes, not personal data
res_data_encrypted: All connections are TLS-encrypted, passwords are hashed with bcrypt
res_data_eu_hosted: Hosted on Hetzner in Germany (EU) — fully GDPR compliant
res_tests_text: The toolbox currently includes: Corsi Block-Tapping (spatial memory), PVT (sustained attention), Tower of London (planning), Raven's Progressive Matrices (non-verbal reasoning), Go/NoGo Stop-Signal (inhibition), AKT (concentration), and WTB (auditory digit span). Each test is configurable — adjust trial counts, timing, and difficulty from the dashboard.
res_propose_suffix: and we'll review it for inclusion.
res_faq_free_a: Yes, completely. psyKasten is open source and free to use for researchers, educators, and students. There are no hidden fees, premium tiers, or usage limits.
res_faq_account_a: No. Participants receive a unique link and can start the test immediately — no sign-up, no download, no app installation required.
res_faq_tests_a: Currently: Corsi Block-Tapping (spatial memory), PVT (sustained attention), Tower of London (planning), Raven's Progressive Matrices (non-verbal reasoning), Go/NoGo Stop-Signal (inhibition), AKT (concentration), and WTB (auditory digit span). More are being added regularly.
res_faq_data_a: All data is stored on Hetzner servers in Germany (EU). No participant IP addresses or browser fingerprints are collected. Connections are encrypted via TLS. The platform is fully GDPR compliant.
res_faq_export_a: You can export results as structured CSV (one row per participant with key metrics), raw CSV (full trial-level data), or JSON. Filter by study, test type, or date range before exporting.
res_faq_participants_q: How many participants can I add?
res_faq_participants_a: There is no hard limit. You can add participants individually or use bulk import to paste up to 500 identifiers at once. Each participant gets their own secure test link.
res_faq_language_q: In which languages are the tests available?
res_faq_language_a: All tests are available in German, English, and Spanish. The test language follows the participant's browser setting, but can also be switched manually.
res_faq_own_test_q: Can I add my own test?
res_faq_own_test_a: Yes! Researchers can propose new tests by uploading a PDF with the test description, methodology, and scoring. Our team reviews proposals and works with you to integrate them into the platform.
res_faq_devices_a: psyKasten works on any device with a modern browser — tablets, laptops, desktops, and smartphones. Tests are optimized for touch screens and run in fullscreen mode to minimize distractions.
res_faq_contact_q: How can I get in touch?
res_faq_contact_a: Send us an email at kontakt@psykasten.de. Whether you have a question, want to report an issue, or are interested in collaborating — we're happy to hear from you.
res_cta_text: Create your free account and set up your first study in minutes.

wcst_description: Test cognitive flexibility by sorting cards according to changing rules
mot_description: Track multiple moving objects to measure visual attention and processing speed
prlt_description: Learn which option is rewarding and adapt when the rules change to measure cognitive flexibility
nback_description: Measure working memory by identifying letters that match N steps back in a sequence
pal_description: A visual memory test measuring the ability to learn pattern-location associations across increasing difficulty levels.
stb_description: Memorize letters and identify whether a probe was in your memory set — measures working memory scanning speed.
tmt_description: Connect numbers (Part A) or alternating numbers and letters (Part B) in order. Measures visual scanning, processing speed, and cognitive flexibility.
bart_description: Behavioral measure of risk-taking propensity. Pump up balloons to earn money, but stop before they pop.
